title: Zwanzig Roughness Asymmetry Creates Bimodal Eigenspectrum in Amyloidogenic Protein MSMs, Quantified by a Comparative Mpemba Index
hypothesis: Amyloidogenic proteins exhibit a bimodal eigenvalue spectrum in their folding/misfolding Markov state models due to the Zwanzig roughness asymmetry (D_fold >> D_misfold), and this bimodality is detectable via a comparative Mpemba index M that classifies amyloid propensity: M >= 2 for amyloidogenic proteins (Abeta42, alpha-synuclein) vs M <= 1 for non-amyloidogenic homologs (Abeta40, beta-synuclein).
mechanism:
Zwanzig (1988, PNAS 85:2029) showed D_eff = D_0*exp(-(epsilon/kT)^2) for rough energy landscapes. Folding on the funneled landscape has low roughness (epsilon_fold ~ 0.5-1.0 kT per minimal frustration principle, Bryngelson & Wolynes 1987 PNAS 84:7524), while misfolding pathways have higher roughness (epsilon_misfold ~ 2-4 kT). Hyeon & Thirumalai (2003, PNAS 100:10249) measured epsilon ~ 2-3 kT for both amyloidogenic and non-amyloidogenic proteins via mechanical unfolding, but this measures OVERALL landscape roughness, not the DIFFERENTIAL roughness between folding and misfolding channels. The hypothesis predicts amyloidogenic proteins have larger (epsilon_misfold - epsilon_fold), creating D_fold/D_misfold ~ exp(8) ~ 3000 for Abeta42.

This diffusivity asymmetry creates bimodal MSM eigenvalue spectra: fast folding modes (tau < 10 us) separated from slow misfolding modes (tau > 100 us). The Mpemba index M (Klich et al. 2019, PRX 9:021060) counts zero-crossings of the overlap coefficient a_2(T) = <P_Boltzmann(T)|v_2> over T in {280,...,400}K at 5K intervals. Prediction: M(Abeta42) >= 2, M(Abeta40) <= 1. Before computing M, eigenvector v_2 must be verified to encode misfolding via DSSP correlation with misregistered beta-sheet content (r > 0.4 required per Jia et al. 2020 PNAS 117:10322).

MSM construction requires de novo building from MD trajectories (e.g., DESRES archives or Folding@Home). Pipeline: TICA featurization (Schwantes & Pande 2013, JCTC 9:2000), k=100-500 microstates, MLE estimation with detailed-balance enforcement (Prinz et al. 2011, J Chem Phys 134:174105), Chapman-Kolmogorov validation, eigendecomposition. Bimodality tested via Hartigan's dip test on the eigenvalue distribution.
bridge: Zwanzig roughness asymmetry (D_fold >> D_misfold) => bimodal MSM eigenspectrum => Mpemba index as diagnostic classifier of amyloid propensity
falsifiable_prediction: (1) MSMs of Abeta42 show bimodal eigenvalue spectrum with > 1 order of magnitude gap between folding and misfolding timescale clusters. (2) M(Abeta42) >= 2 while M(Abeta40) <= 1. (3) v_2 of Abeta42 MSM has Pearson r > 0.4 with misregistered beta-sheet content.
test_protocol: 1. Obtain >100 us aggregate MD trajectory for Abeta42 and Abeta40. 2. Construct MSMs using PyEMMA with TICA. 3. Eigendecompose; test bimodality via Hartigan's dip test. 4. Compute a_2(T) over 280-400K; count zero-crossings. 5. Validate v_2 identity via DSSP. Timeline: 3-6 months with existing trajectories.

grounded_claims.claim: Zwanzig (1988) D_eff = D_0*exp(-(epsilon/kT)^2) for rough landscapes
Klich et al. 2019 Mpemba index definition and spectral mechanism
Hyeon & Thirumalai 2003 roughness epsilon ~ 2-3 kT via mechanical unfolding
Bryngelson & Wolynes 1987 minimal frustration principle
Jia et al. 2020 misregistered kinetic traps in amyloid MSM
Prinz et al. 2011 MSM estimation methodology
Scherer et al. 2015 PyEMMA software
D_fold/D_misfold ratio ~ 3000 for Abeta42
grounded_claims.source: Derived from Zwanzig formula; never measured

grounded_claims.claim: Bimodal eigenvalue spectrum in amyloidogenic protein MSMs
M(Abeta42) >= 2 vs M(Abeta40) <= 1
DESRES Abeta42 trajectory public availability
grounded_claims.source: Uncertain; DESRES has released some trajectories

counter_evidence: Hyeon & Thirumalai 2003 found similar ABSOLUTE roughness for amyloidogenic and non-amyloidogenic proteins -- differential roughness may not discriminate
Zwanzig formula is for 1D periodic potential; proteins are 10^3-10^5 dimensional
Standard MSMs enforce detailed balance (normal matrix), potentially eliminating interesting non-normal spectral features
Bimodal eigenvalue structure is untested keystone prediction -- if it fails, framework collapses
v_2 might encode slow conformational process unrelated to aggregation (e.g., proline isomerization)
changes_from_parent: Consolidated H5 (physical basis) and H1 (diagnostic) into single two-level hypothesis. Removed ALL fabricated MSM citations (Rosenman, Robustelli, Eschmann). Acknowledged MSMs must be constructed de novo. Added eigenmode identity verification (DSSP correlation). Added Hyeon & Thirumalai counter-evidence for differential roughness. Specified DIFFERENTIAL roughness (not absolute) as discriminating variable. Added Hartigan dip test for bimodality. Corrected protein pair choices (Abeta40/beta-synuclein as controls).

title: Cooling-Rate-Controlled Eigenmode Overlap Bypassing Modulates Abeta42 Aggregation-Competent Conformer Flux
hypothesis: Different cooling rates from 50C to 37C produce Abeta42 monomer ensembles with different overlap onto the slowest MSM eigenmode, creating non-monotonic dependence of aggregation lag time on cooling rate via differential supply of aggregation-competent conformers feeding primary nucleation.
mechanism:
The Mpemba effect core mechanism (Klich et al. 2019 PRX 9:021060): different initial conditions produce different overlaps a_2 = <P_prepared|v_2> with the slowest eigenmode. If v_2 encodes the aggregation-competent basin, controlling a_2 controls the monomer supply rate to primary nucleation. CRITICAL REFRAMING: Cycle 1 H4 falsely cited Kusumoto 1998 as non-monotonic. Kusumoto et al. (1998, PNAS 95:12277) show MONOTONIC Arrhenius elongation (E_A = 23 kcal/mol). This hypothesis does NOT require non-monotonic aggregation kinetics -- it requires non-monotonic eigenmode OVERLAP as function of cooling rate, a different observable.

The overlap a_2(r_cool) depends on how the Boltzmann distribution at T_start traverses non-equilibrium states during cooling. Even with monotonic Arrhenius kinetics, the conformer distribution reaching T_final depends on the cooling path. DISTINGUISHING PREDICTION: if Arrhenius only, lag time decreases monotonically with slower cooling (more time at elevated T). If eigenmode-overlap bypassing operates, lag time shows NON-MONOTONIC dependence with a maximum at some r*.

MONOMER-TO-AGGREGATION BRIDGE: c_competent(0) determined by eigenmode overlap enters the Knowles-Cohen-Vendruscolo framework (Cohen et al. 2012 PNAS 109:9761) through primary nucleation rate k_n. Effect is most visible in lag phase (before secondary nucleation overwhelms). Predicted effect size: 2-5x change in lag time. Protocol: 6-point cooling rate scan (0.1-30 C/min) from 50C to 37C with ThT, CD, ANS. Jonckheere-Terpstra test for non-monotonicity.
bridge: Mpemba eigenmode-overlap control via cooling rate => non-monotonic supply of aggregation-competent Abeta42 monomers => modulation of primary nucleation lag phase
falsifiable_prediction: (1) Abeta42 fibril formation lag time shows non-monotonic dependence on cooling rate across 6-point scan. (2) Non-monotonicity is specific to lag phase; growth phase rate is rate-independent. (3) CD spectra at t=0 show rate-dependent beta-sheet content differences at the lag-time-maximizing rate r*.
test_protocol: 1. Prepare monomerized Abeta42 (SEC purification). 2. Heat to 50C, hold 10 min, cool to 37C at 6 rates (0.1, 0.3, 1.0, 3.0, 10.0, 30.0 C/min). 3. ThT kinetics at 37C (20 uM Abeta42, triplicate). 4. CD spectra at t=0, 2h, 6h, 24h. 5. Fit lag time per Arosio et al. 2015 (PCCP 17:7606). 6. Jonckheere-Terpstra test + quadratic fit. Timeline: 2-3 months.

grounded_claims.claim: Klich 2019 eigenmode overlap mechanism
Kusumoto 1998 shows MONOTONIC Arrhenius kinetics for Abeta42 elongation
Cohen et al. 2012 secondary nucleation dominance
Arosio et al. 2015 lag time analysis protocol
Abeta42 IDP conformational ensemble changes substantially between 37C and 50C
2-5x effect size on lag time from cooling rate variation
Non-monotonic cooling-rate dependence of aggregation lag time

counter_evidence: Abeta42 is IDP -- conformational ensemble at 50C vs 37C may differ less than for globular proteins
ThT kinetics variability (CV > 30%) may mask the non-monotonic signal
Secondary nucleation dominates proliferation; primary nucleation effect may be overwhelmed
Simpler explanation: rapid quench just minimizes time at elevated T (Occam's razor)
c_competent concept assumes discrete aggregation-competent subensemble, may oversimplify continuous IDP landscape
changes_from_parent: Removed false Kusumoto 1998 attribution. Rebuilt argument: non-monotonic eigenmode OVERLAP is distinct from monotonic aggregation RATE. Lowered T_start from 60C to 50C. Added explicit monomer-to-aggregation bridge via c_competent and KCV framework. Added Jonckheere-Terpstra statistical test. Reframed as modulating monomer supply rate, not total fibril mass.

title: Multi-Eigenmode Branching at Distinct Cooling Rates Determines Abeta42 Fibril Polymorph Distribution
hypothesis: If the Abeta42 MSM has multiple slow eigenmodes (v_2, v_3) each encoding a distinct polymorph precursor basin, then different cooling rates from 50C to 37C produce different a_2/a_3 overlap ratios, directing monomer flux preferentially toward one polymorph or another, detectable by cryo-EM polymorph classification.
mechanism:
Abeta42 fibrils exhibit structural polymorphism (Gremer et al. 2017, Science 358:116; multiple cryo-EM structures). This hypothesis proposes that polymorph selection is kinetically controlled by the multi-eigenmode branching ratio a_2/a_3 in the monomer MSM. If v_2 and v_3 encode distinct conformational basins corresponding to distinct monomer-monomer contact patterns (misregistered beta-sheet contacts per Jia et al. 2020 PNAS 117:10322), then different initial conditions (cooling rates) produce different a_2/a_3 ratios, directing nucleation toward different polymorph precursors.

Distinct from C2-H2: total fibril mass may be SIMILAR across cooling rates (within 25%), but polymorph DISTRIBUTION differs. Prerequisite: MSM must have tau_2 and tau_3 within a factor of ~10 of each other (both slow relative to folding). If tau_3 >> 100*tau_2, the third mode decays too fast to influence polymorph selection.

Monomer-to-fibril polymorph bridge: different monomer conformational sub-ensembles template different fibril cores. Jia et al. 2020 showed misregistered contacts are dominant kinetic traps. v_2 may encode one registration pattern, v_3 another. The eigenmode branching ratio then determines which registration dominates the nucleus.
bridge: Multi-eigenmode branching ratio (a_2/a_3 controlled by cooling rate) => differential monomer flux to distinct polymorph precursor basins => cryo-EM-detectable polymorph distribution shift
falsifiable_prediction: (1) Abeta42 fibril polymorph distribution depends on cooling rate from 50C to 37C. (2) Total fibril mass at 72h is within 25% across rates; polymorph fractions shift by > 20%. (3) MSM prerequisite: tau_2/tau_3 < 100.
test_protocol: 1. Aggregate Abeta42 at 37C after cooling from 50C at 3 rates (0.3, 3.0, 30.0 C/min). 2. At 72h, harvest, vitrify on EM grids. 3. Cryo-EM 2D classification to identify distinct morphologies. 4. Chi-squared test of polymorph frequency across conditions. 5. Parallel: construct MSM, verify tau_2/tau_3 < 100. Timeline: 6-12 months.

grounded_claims.claim: Gremer et al. 2017 Abeta42 cryo-EM fibril structure
Jia et al. 2020 misregistered kinetic traps
Klich 2019 eigenmode overlap formalism
Abeta42 MSM has tau_2/tau_3 < 100
Monomer conformational sub-ensembles template distinct fibril core structures
Multi-eigenmode branching ratio determines polymorph selection

counter_evidence: Polymorph selection may be determined by ELONGATION conditions, not initial monomer ensemble
Fibrils may restructure during elongation, losing memory of initial monomer conformation
Only 1-2 dominant in vitro morphologies may be detectable by cryo-EM
The computational prerequisite (tau_2/tau_3 < 100) may not hold
pH, salt, and agitation are stronger polymorph determinants; cooling rate may be undetectable
changes_from_parent: Abandoned PrP system (no constructable MSM, denaturation at 65C). Pivoted to Abeta42 polymorphs (cryo-EM structures available). Removed 80C starting temperature; replaced with 50C. Added monomer-to-polymorph bridge via Jia 2020 misregistered contacts. Added computational prerequisite (tau_2/tau_3 < 100) as built-in falsification.

title: Monomer Eigenspectral Properties Set the Ceiling on Primary Nucleation Rate Constants in the Knowles-Cohen-Vendruscolo Amyloid Kinetic Framework
hypothesis: The MSM spectral gap and aggregation-competent basin population of amyloidogenic monomers set an upper bound on the primary nucleation rate constant k_n: k_n <= C * pi_competent * gamma. This bound is tight at low concentrations (primary-nucleation-dominated) and loose at high concentrations (secondary-nucleation-dominated), creating a quantitative bridge between monomer conformational dynamics and multi-molecule aggregation kinetics.
mechanism:
This directly addresses the Critic's central question (Q2): how does single-molecule MSM predict multi-molecule aggregation? The answer: it provides an UPPER BOUND on k_n, not a direct prediction. Step 1: Define aggregation-competent conformers as MSM microstates with beta-strand exposure exceeding threshold (DSSP-based, per Jia et al. 2020 PNAS 117:10322). Step 2: The flux from non-competent to competent states is bounded by spectral gap gamma = 1 - lambda_2 and equilibrium competent population pi_competent. J_ss = pi_competent * gamma (simplified; committor-based correction may refine). Step 3: k_n <= C * J_ss where C encodes concentration-dependent collision rate.

Cross-protein prediction: plot experimentally measured k_n (from KCV fits per Meisl et al. 2016 Nature Protocols 11:252) against pi_competent * gamma for Abeta42, Abeta40, alpha-synuclein, IAPP. All points should fall below diagonal (inequality) with Spearman rho > 0.5. Critical: bound is TIGHT at low concentration (primary nucleation bottleneck) but LOOSE when secondary nucleation dominates (Cohen et al. 2012 PNAS 109:9761). This selective applicability distinguishes from naive MSM-aggregation correlation.
bridge: MSM spectral gap and competent-basin population => upper bound on aggregation-competent conformer flux => ceiling on primary nucleation rate k_n in KCV framework
falsifiable_prediction: (1) For 4+ amyloid proteins, k_n correlates positively with pi_competent * gamma (rho > 0.5). (2) All k_n values satisfy inequality k_n <= C * pi_competent * gamma for single C. (3) Bound is tight at low [monomer], loose at high [monomer] or with seeds.
test_protocol: 1. Construct MSMs for Abeta42, Abeta40, alpha-synuclein, IAPP. 2. Compute gamma and pi_competent per DSSP threshold. 3. Retrieve published k_n from KCV fits (Meisl 2016). 4. Plot k_n vs pi_competent * gamma; test correlation and inequality. 5. Repeat at 2+ concentrations. Timeline: 6-12 months.

grounded_claims.claim: Cohen et al. 2012 secondary nucleation dominance
Knowles et al. 2009 amyloid kinetic framework
Meisl et al. 2016 kinetic fitting protocol
Jia et al. 2020 aggregation-competent conformer identification
Husic & Pande 2018 MSM eigenspectral analysis
J_ss = pi_competent * gamma as flux formula
grounded_claims.source: Simplified from MSM theory; may need committor correction

grounded_claims.claim: k_n <= C * J_ss inequality
grounded_claims.source: Physically motivated but not rigorously derived

grounded_claims.claim: Spectral-gap-bounded nucleation rate framework

counter_evidence: Conformational interconversion may be FAST relative to nucleation -- collision kinetics, not monomer dynamics, may be bottleneck
4-protein panel allows rho > 0.5 by chance 17% of the time
pi_competent depends on arbitrary DSSP threshold choice
Published k_n values have large uncertainties (order-of-magnitude)
For Abeta42, k_2 >> k_n by factor >10^4 (Cohen 2013 PNAS 110:9882), making k_n bound correct but practically unimportant
changes_from_parent: NEW hypothesis directly addressing Critic Q2 (monomer-to-aggregation gap). Proposes first quantitative link between MSM spectral properties and KCV rate constants. Explicitly acknowledges selectivity: bound matters only when primary nucleation is bottleneck.

title: Irreversible Aggregation Sink States Break Detailed Balance in Amyloid MSMs, Enabling Non-Normal Spectral Dynamics and Transient Misfolding Acceleration
hypothesis: Adding irreversible aggregation absorbing states to reversible protein MSMs breaks detailed balance, producing a non-normal transition matrix T_agg whose pseudospectral properties create transient amplification of aggregation-competent conformer populations. The Kreiss constant K(T_agg) quantifies this amplification and correlates with experimentally measured aggregation lag times.
mechanism:
Cycle 1 H3 was KILLED because standard MSMs enforce detailed balance (normal matrix). This hypothesis specifies HOW detailed balance is broken: irreversible aggregation sinks. When monomers bind growing fibrils, the transition is effectively irreversible on conformational timescales. Mathematically: T_agg = T_rev + A, where A encodes sparse irreversible transitions from competent states to absorbing 'aggregated' state. T_agg violates detailed balance and is generally NON-NORMAL.

Non-normal matrices exhibit transient amplification (Trefethen & Embree 2005, 'Spectra and Pseudospectra'): even with all eigenvalues <= 1, the pseudospectrum extends beyond the unit disk, creating transient growth of certain observables. In protein context: aggregation-competent conformer population can transiently EXCEED equilibrium before decaying. This overshoot provides elevated nucleation probability window.

Biological significance: explains threshold-like nucleation (long lag then rapid growth). During lag phase, monomer ensemble is in transient amplification regime. Once nucleus forms during overshoot, secondary nucleation takes over. Construction: augment standard MSM with absorbing state using p_agg(i) = k_on * c_monomer * lag_time * f_competent(i), where k_on ~ 10^4 M^-1 s^-1 for Abeta42 (Cohen 2012). Compute pseudospectrum and Kreiss constant K = max_z(||(zI-T_agg)^{-1}|| * (|z|-1)) for |z|>1. Prediction: K(Abeta42) > K(Abeta40).
bridge: Irreversible aggregation sinks break detailed balance => non-normal T_agg matrix => transient amplification of aggregation-competent conformer population => threshold-like nucleation behavior
falsifiable_prediction: (1) T_agg for amyloidogenic proteins has Kreiss constant K > 10; reversible T_rev has K = 1. (2) K(Abeta42) > K(Abeta40). (3) Pseudospectral radius decay time correlates with experimental lag times across protein panel.
test_protocol: 1. Construct reversible MSMs for Abeta42 and Abeta40. 2. Augment with irreversible sink (estimate p_agg from published k_on). 3. Compute pseudospectrum and Kreiss constant. 4. Compare K(Abeta42) vs K(Abeta40). 5. Compare decay time with experimental lag phases. Timeline: 4-8 months (purely computational).

grounded_claims.claim: Trefethen & Embree 2005 non-normal matrix dynamics
Cohen et al. 2012 k_on order of magnitude for Abeta42
Klich 2019 and Teza 2025 non-normal Liouvillian dynamics
Standard MSMs enforce detailed balance producing normal matrices
Kreiss constant K > 10 for amyloidogenic T_agg
p_agg estimation from bimolecular collision rate
Non-reversible MSM construction for amyloid systems
Transient amplification explains threshold nucleation

counter_evidence: p_agg values are highly uncertain and concentration-dependent; non-normal structure depends on these
Adding absorbing states fundamentally changes dynamics; may not be well-described by time-homogeneous Markov chain
Transient amplification may be trivial depletion effect, not genuine non-normal eigenvector mechanism
Kreiss constant is upper bound; actual amplification may be much smaller
Initial condition may not align with amplification direction in practice
changes_from_parent: NEW hypothesis rescuing KILLED H3 (non-normal dynamics). Specifies concrete mechanism for detailed-balance violation: irreversible aggregation sink states. Uses established non-normal matrix theory (Trefethen & Embree). Provides computable metric (Kreiss constant) for testing. Connects to multi-molecule aggregation through the sink-state mechanism itself.

title: Mpemba-Optimal Thermal Cycling Protocol for Amyloid Seed Amplification Assays (RT-QuIC/PMCA) Derived from MSM Eigenspectral Analysis
hypothesis: The sensitivity of RT-QuIC and PMCA amyloid seed amplification assays can be improved by designing temperature cycling protocols that minimize the overlap coefficient |a_2| between the substrate protein's conformational ensemble and its slowest MSM eigenmode, achieving the Mpemba condition for exponentially faster re-equilibration to aggregation-competent distributions per cycle.
mechanism:
RT-QuIC and PMCA are diagnostic assays detecting trace amyloid seeds by iterative cycles of incubation and agitation/sonication. Temperature cycling parameters (T_high, T_low, dwell times, ramp rates) are empirically optimized with no theoretical framework. This hypothesis proposes that the Mpemba eigenspectral framework (Klich et al. 2019 PRX 9:021060) provides principled optimization.

Each temperature cycle takes the substrate protein through non-equilibrium relaxation. Seed amplification efficiency depends on how rapidly the substrate re-equilibrates to the aggregation-promoting distribution after each perturbation. If the cycling protocol achieves the Mpemba condition (a_2 ~ 0: minimal overlap with slowest eigenmode), re-equilibration is exponentially faster, producing more aggregation-competent substrate per cycle. For two-temperature cycling (T_high, T_low) at ramp rate r: a_2(T_high, r) = <P(T_high, r)|v_2(T_low)>. Optimize: min_{T_high, r} |a_2|. Predicted speedup: time-to-threshold decreases by factor tau_2/tau_3 (2-10x).

Translational significance: RT-QuIC for synucleinopathies (Parkinson's, DLB, MSA) achieves 80-95% sensitivity. A 2-5x improvement in time-to-threshold enables faster diagnosis and detection of lower seed concentrations from more accessible samples (nasal brushings, skin biopsies).
bridge: Mpemba eigenmode-overlap optimization => principled temperature cycling for RT-QuIC/PMCA => improved sensitivity and speed of amyloid seed amplification diagnostics
falsifiable_prediction: (1) MSM eigenspectral analysis identifies specific (T_high*, r*) minimizing |a_2| for recombinant alpha-synuclein. (2) RT-QuIC with Mpemba-optimal protocol shows > 30% shorter time-to-threshold vs standard 42C protocol at 10^-8 seed dilution. (3) Improvement is specific to lag phase, not final ThT plateau.
test_protocol: 1. Construct alpha-synuclein MSM. 2. Compute a_2(T_high, r) surface over T_high in {40,42,45,48,50,55}C and r in {0.5,1,5,10,50} C/min. 3. Identify Mpemba-optimal (T_high*, r*). 4. Run alpha-synuclein RT-QuIC: standard vs Mpemba-optimal vs matched-temperature control (n=8 per condition, 10^-8 and 10^-10 dilutions). 5. One-sided t-test for reduced time-to-threshold. Timeline: 3-6 months.

grounded_claims.claim: Klich 2019 eigenmode overlap optimization formalism
RT-QuIC as established clinical diagnostic for CJD
Alpha-synuclein RT-QuIC being developed for PD diagnosis
RT-QuIC sensitivity 80-95% for synucleinopathies
tau_2/tau_3 ratio 2-10 for alpha-synuclein
Mpemba-optimized temperature cycling for seed amplification

counter_evidence: RT-QuIC amplification may be dominated by mechanical fragmentation, not conformational dynamics
Assay environment (plate reader, ThT, detergent, beads) is far from clean MD conditions
Mpemba-optimal protocol may require impractical temperatures
Alpha-synuclein MSMs may not exist with sufficient accuracy
Current protocols are already extensively empirically optimized and may be near the practical maximum
changes_from_parent: NEW translational hypothesis applying core Mpemba-amyloid framework to clinical diagnostics. Provides distinct application layer beyond the mechanistic/diagnostic hierarchy.
